title: [BUG] Custom date formats cannot be parsed when streaming reading Excel
author: Jun-Ao
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

Lib version: 4.4.0

## Steps To Reproduce

When I format a cell as a custom format in Excel, I can only get a number instead of a date when I parse this cell with streaming reading.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6a033462-53fd-4a7b-aa47-111a6bccf871)

After debugging, I found that the problem is in the following code
```
// lib/xlsx/xform/style/numfmt-xform.js
NumFmtXform.getDefaultFmtCode = function getDefaultFmtCode(numFmtId) {
  return defaultNumFormats[numFmtId] && defaultNumFormats[numFmtId].f;
};
```
And the content of this defaultNumFormats is as follows:
```
// lib/xlsx/defaultnumformats.js
module.exports = {
  ......
  14: {f: 'mm-dd-yy'},
  ......
  31: {
    'zh-tw': 'yyyy"年"m"月"d"日"',
    'zh-cn': 'yyyy"年"m"月"d"日"',
    'ja-jp': 'yyyy"年"m"月"d"日"',
    'ko-kr': 'yyyy"년" mm"월" dd"일"',
  },
  ......
}
```
It should be noted that the default structure is different for numFmtId of 14 and 31.
This code returns 'mm-dd-yy' when numFmtId is 14(address: A2) and undefined when numFmtId is 31(Address: A4), which results in a cell with numFmtId 27 being considered unformatted and just a number

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09b7a054-2114-44d1-8947-8b8f3f5a4c34)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19c363a3-4b58-4c79-96f9-b9965f12394a)

parsed result:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3878977-2574-4da2-b3c7-5f8a123bb7cf)

## My question:

I probably know the cause of the problem, but I'm not sure how to solve it:
1. How to obtain the location of the file to determine the use of zh-tw/zh-cn/ja-jp/ko-kr to replace the f attribute
2. Will this affect streaming write, non-streaming read and write after I adjust it?
3. Is the content in lib/xlsx/defaultnumformats.js complete?

## The expected behaviour:

I want to parse the date in the custom format correctly, instead of getting the number

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

My current solution is to simply get the f attribute or the zh-cn attribute, because I only need to judge whether the content of this cell is a date, but if someone can provide a better way, I would be very grateful
```
defaultNumFormats[numFmtId] && (defaultNumFormats[numFmtId].f || defaultNumFormats[numFmtId]['zh-cn']
```
## file:
I guess any custom date format can reproduce this problem
[wode.xlsx](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18978456/wode.xlsx)

comments.author: noah227
comments.body:
To get the date instead of a number, refer to the [datevalue-function#remarks](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/datevalue-function-df8b07d4-7761-4a93-bc33-b7471bbff252). 
[excel-date-to-js](https://www.npmjs.com/package/excel-date-to-js) works well, btw.
